heroHeadline: Artemis II Completes Historic Lunar Flyby — Farthest Humans From Earth Since Apollo
heroSubtitle: Flight Day 6 (Apr 6): Orion flew within 4,070 miles of the Moon at 7:02 p.m. EDT and set a crewed distance record of 252,706 miles from Earth — surpassing Apollo 13's 1970 record. Crew (Wiseman, Glover, Koch, Hansen) photographed 30 lunar surface targets. Mission on track for return and splashdown.
footerNote: Data sourced from NASA.gov, OIG Reports, SpaceNews, Ars Technica, Congressional appropriations. Tier 1: NASA/ESA/Official. Tier 2: SpaceNews/Ars Technica/Reuters. Tier 3: Planetary Society/AIAA.
